title: Private docker registry - skopeo copies one container from quay.io, fails on another
body:
Failure:
```skopeo --debug copy --dest-tls-verify=false docker://quay.io/external_storage/nfs-client-provisioner:v3.1.0-k8s1.11 docker://rmt.example.com:5000/quay.io/nfs-client-provisioner:v3.1.0-k8s1.11

DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]   Using file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore

DEBU[0000]  crt: /etc/docker/certs.d/rmt.example.com:5000/domain.crt

```

What might be the reason behind?

comments.body: Could you document the exact skopeo command you are executing?

Thanks. I was absolutely sure that I also entered the actual commands, but they seem to have gone missing.
(Edit: The command was not displayed because using ` ` ` only starts withe next line.)

This fails:
```
skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false docker://quay.io/external_storage/nfs-client-provisioner:v3.1.0-k8s1.11 docker://rmt.example.com:5000/quay.io/nfs-client-provisioner:v3.1.0-k8s1.11 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 36dfde95678a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a073c86ecf9e skipped: already exists  
Copying blob d9d714ee28a7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 .                                        
Writing manifest to image destination
FATA[0010] Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest v3.1.0-k8s1.11 to rmt.example.com:5000/quay.io/nfs-client-provisioner: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error 
```

This works:
```
skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false docker://quay.io/kubernetes-ingress-controller/nginx-ingress-controller:0.33.0 docker://rmt.example.com:5000/quay.io/kubernetes-ingress-controller/nginx-ingress-controller:0.33.0
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob cbdbe7a5bc2a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 42d2a0e40de7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 8914224c9c90 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 11f3b8b7eea2 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob b306c26b2152 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 0e937a600754 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 8a92de035314 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 09a3a6adb7ed skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 966ec1120b67 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 59fc0191cff0 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob c3c2bd8bcea6 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 7f21ee2ccc91 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 1b7b41b3144e skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 24b8b0282442 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 1b47f1fd101a [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
And the top output is with `--debug`

It looks like the registry doesn't like manifest (it seems to always fail there). `docker://quay.io/external_storage/nfs-client-provisioner:v3.1.0-k8s1.11` is a schema1 image which might be rejected by ther other registry.

Can you try with `skopeo copy --format v2s2 ...`?

That works. 
It also works without the `--format` option with version `0.1.39.`

Thanks!
Really the primary answer should be to read the corresponding registry server logs. Nothing but the actual registry software knows what an “internal server error” is.

I'm not sure if I would have understood the logs.
Imagev1 would not work, but format v2s2 did. :)

I do admit I am far from an expert on the subject, since I just needed to run the command a few times a year to have an image at hand, and suddenly one day it changed for one of the images from the same registry. That's the reason I needed help from you guys.  

Anyway humble thanks and apologies!
Great that it works! Thanks for reaching out, @phakkarainen

Hm… too late, but a guess: docker/distribution having schema1 disabled by default (https://github.com/docker/distribution/issues/2963 and so on) + no way to automatically handle that (https://github.com/docker/distribution/issues/2925 / https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/272 ). In that case either forcing v2s2, or re-enabling schema1 at the registry, would be closest to “the right” fix.

Thanks Miloslav, good information!

I see 3 players in this game: remote registry, skopeo CLI, and local registry.

Does either of the registries make a difference when with skopeo version 0.39 copy works, with 0.41 it fails?

Hum, that’s a good point, the disabled schema1 should fail with all versions.

The code did change fairly extensively between 0.1.39 and 0.1.41, but nothing obviously sticks out.

Anyway, no point in guessing. The direct next step still is “read the corresponding server logs”.

This is the local docker log with `--details`. Points to schema, yes. 
```

```

Huh, `err.detail="manifest schema v1 unsupported"` == `ErrSchemaV1Unsupported` is the “schema1 was turned off” error. (BTW that can be re-enabled server side, so that you don’t have to use `--format v2s2`.)

I can’t see how 0.1.39 can work.

Two possibilities:
1) I deleted the newer skopeo, installed the previous. (Did I not, then?)
2) I tried another system with another version of docker. (Must be the situation.)
